meaning: adj.未说过的,未透露的,数不清的
examples: These gases cause untold damage to the environment.
An Olympic gold medal can lead to untold riches for an athlete.
Harry Potter and the Cursed Child will tell the "untold part" of the boy wizard's story, Rowling has said.
We knew that there was a bigger story out there that was still untold.
World War II simply burned up untold quantities of every resource, human, mineral, and environmental.
